meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Proof by contradiction for sqrt(2)
meta-distractor.value: Assume sqrt(2) = 2, then 2 = 4, which is impossible.
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Assume sqrt(2) = 2, then 2 = 4, which is impossible.', 'sqrt(2) is not 2 to begin with. This sets up an irrelevant contradiction, not the standard rationality argument.'],

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: The inductive step structure
meta-distractor.value: Assume the statement holds for all n, then check n = k
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: miss('Assume the statement holds for all n, then check n = k', 'Assuming it for all n is circular; you assume only the single case n = k.', 'The hypothesis is for one specific k, not for every n at once.'),

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Base case of induction
meta-distractor.value: Assume true for n = k
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: miss('Assume true for n = k', 'This is the inductive hypothesis step, not the base case; the base case verifies the smallest value directly.', 'The base case checks a specific starting value, not an assumption.'),

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor

meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: miss('Ignore it, since a few percent imbalance is normal random variation', 'A statistically improbable split signals a bug, not normal variation, and can bias the whole result.', 'The chi-squared test already says this is not chance.'),
